expectations.evidence: Lines 3-10 clearly identify heap fragmentation from repeated new/delete as the root cause, explaining how 40+ alloc/free cycles per second cause fragmentation over hours.
Replaces new SensorReading() with stack-local/static struct and new String() with static char buffers. Complete refactored code uses zero heap allocations in steady state.
Explicitly replaces Arduino String with static char[] + snprintf throughout. Includes before/after code and a table of common String patterns with their replacements.
Provides concrete monitor_heap() function using ESP.getFreeHeap() and ESP.getMaxAllocHeap(), with fragmentation percentage calculation and alert thresholds.
Includes multiple concrete code blocks: ObjectPool template, stack-local approach, snprintf-based publish, and a complete refactored sensor_task_loop() with heap monitoring.
Recommends static buffers, stack locals, object pools, and StaticJsonDocument, but never mentions ETL (etl::string, etl::vector) or any other named fixed-capacity container library.
Explains why dynamic allocation is harmful in embedded contexts but does not reference MISRA rules or any formal embedded coding standard.
Calculates 4 sensors x 10Hz = 40 allocations/second for readings, ~10/second for MQTT strings, totaling ~1,080,000 allocations over 6 hours.
